content.name: AIAP Auto Evolution Validator v1.2.0

thinking_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.summary: Post-round quality validator for automated multi-round AIAP evolution. Compares pre-round baseline against post-round results, detects quality regression across all dimensions, decides pass/fail/rollback.
content.description: Validator module for AIAP Auto Evolution. Called after each round to evaluate evolution quality. Flow: LoadBaseline -> CompareQuality -> CheckRegression -> (PassRound | AnalyzeRegression -> DecideAction -> SuggestFix) | FailRound. 8 nodes.

pre_round_baseline.agentic_prompt: Quality baseline before this round's evolution.

target_program_path.agentic_prompt: Absolute path to target AIAP program directory.

content.system_prompt:
Validate evolution quality. Detect regression. Protect against quality degradation across rounds. Be strict but fair.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[Validate Request] --> LoadBaseline[Load Pre-Round Baseline]
    LoadBaseline --> CompareQuality[Compare Quality Scores]
    CompareQuality --> CheckRegression{Regression Check}
    CheckRegression -- No Regression --> PassRound[Pass Round]
    CheckRegression -- Regression --> AnalyzeRegression[Analyze Regression]
    AnalyzeRegression --> DecideAction{Decide Action}
    DecideAction -- Recoverable --> SuggestFix[Suggest Fix]
    DecideAction -- Unrecoverable --> FailRound[Fail Round]
    PassRound --> endNode((End))
    SuggestFix --> endNode((End))
    FailRound --> endNode((End))

Start.step1: Receive validation request with pre_round_baseline, execution_result, target_program_path.
Start.constraints: All parameters required. execution_result must contain quality_score.
Start.Error: Missing quality_score in execution_result -> treat as validation failure.

LoadBaseline.step1: Extract pre-round quality metrics from pre_round_baseline: { weighted_score, grade, C_score, I_score, D_score, simulation_pass_rate, node_count }.
LoadBaseline.step2: Extract post-round quality metrics from execution_result.quality_score: { weighted_score, grade, C_score, I_score, D_score }. Extract simulation from execution_result.simulation_result: { scenarios, passed, pass_rate }.
LoadBaseline.step3: If execution_result.success = false: skip quality comparison, immediately route to FailRound with reason 'Creator pipeline failed: {execution_result.error}'.
LoadBaseline.step3_5: DUAL-FORMAT VALIDATION (B2 v1.2.0 — AISOP/AISIP protocol alignment): If target program uses DUAL format (both .aisop.json and .aisip.json): (1) Verify both file sets were updated by Creator — if only one format was modified, emit WARNING: 'DUAL-INCOMPLETE: Creator only modified {format} files. Other format may be out of sync.' (2) CROSS-FORMAT CONSISTENCY: for each module, verify that function definitions in .aisop.json match those in .aisip.json (§4 topology differs but functions should be identical). Mismatch → YELLOW: 'DUAL-DRIFT: module {name} has function differences between AISOP and AISIP variants.' (3) VERSION CONSISTENCY: verify version field matches across all files of both formats. (4) SKIP: if target is single-format (AISOP only or AISIP only), skip dual-format checks entirely. LOG: 'DUAL-FORMAT: {status}'.
LoadBaseline.constraints: Execution failure is an automatic FAIL — no quality comparison needed. Dual-format checks are advisory (YELLOW) — do not fail round for format drift.
LoadBaseline.Error: Cannot load baseline -> use AIAP.md quality fields as fallback.

CompareQuality.step1: Compute quality deltas: (a) weighted_delta = post.weighted_score - pre.weighted_score. (b) C_delta = post.C - pre.C. (c) I_delta = post.I - pre.I. (d) D_delta = post.D - pre.D. (e) simulation_delta = post.pass_rate - pre.pass_rate.
CompareQuality.step2: Compute overall_assessment: (a) ALL deltas >= 0 -> 'improved' or 'maintained'. (b) weighted_delta >= 0 BUT some sub-dimension < 0 -> 'mixed'. (c) weighted_delta < 0 -> 'regressed'.
CompareQuality.step3: Output quality_comparison = { weighted_delta, C_delta, I_delta, D_delta, simulation_delta, overall_assessment }.
CompareQuality.constraints: Use exact numeric comparison. Delta of 0.000 is 'maintained' (acceptable).
CompareQuality.Error: Comparison fails -> treat as 'unknown' -> route to CheckRegression as regression for safety.

CheckRegression.step1: Evaluate quality_comparison: (a) overall_assessment = 'improved' or 'maintained' -> No Regression -> PassRound. (b) overall_assessment = 'mixed' -> check severity: if regressed dimension delta > -0.1 AND weighted_delta >= 0 -> No Regression (minor acceptable). Else -> Regression. (c) overall_assessment = 'regressed' -> Regression. (d) simulation pass_rate decreased -> Regression regardless of score.
CheckRegression.step1_5: CROSS-ROUND QUALITY TREND ANALYSIS (B1 v1.1.0 — AtScale 2025 'AI amplified semantic drift' cumulative detection; OpenAI 2025 Self-Evolving Agents multi-iteration baseline tracking; InfoQ 2025 Pipeline Quality Gates trend-aware gating): After single-round regression evaluation (step1) and BEFORE routing decision, perform MULTI-ROUND quality trend analysis using accumulated round history. PURPOSE: detect slow, cumulative quality degradation that individual single-round checks miss — the 'boiling frog' problem where each round shows minor acceptable changes that compound into significant quality loss. DEPENDENCY: requires round_state.completed_rounds from pipeline context (passed via execution_result or separate parameter). If unavailable or < 3 completed rounds, SKIP trend analysis (insufficient history). METHOD: (1) GRADUAL DECLINE DETECTION: extract weighted_score from each completed round's quality record. If last 3+ rounds each show individual delta ≤ -0.05 (within single-round tolerance) but cumulative delta across all 3 exceeds -0.10 → emit gradual_decline = { detected: true, individual_deltas: [float], cumulative_delta: float, rounds_declining: int, message: 'Quality declining gradually: each round within tolerance but cumulative drop of {cumulative} across {N} rounds exceeds threshold.' }. (2) CONVERGENCE DETECTION: if last 3+ rounds each show |delta| < 0.001 → emit convergence = { detected: true, stable_score: float, stable_rounds: int, message: 'Quality converged at {score} for {N} rounds. Further evolution unlikely to yield improvement.' }. (3) DIMENSION DIVERGENCE: for each dimension (C, I, D), extract per-round deltas. If dimension A shows positive trend (improving) while dimension B shows negative trend (declining) across 2+ consecutive rounds → emit dimension_divergence = { detected: true, improving: [{ dimension, trend: float }], declining: [{ dimension, trend: float }], message: '{dim_up} improving (+{delta_up}) but {dim_down} declining ({delta_down}) — evolution may be optimizing one dimension at the expense of another.' }. (4) AGGREGATE: trend_analysis = { trend_health: 'healthy' (0 detections) | 'warning' (1 detection) | 'critical' (2+ detections), gradual_decline: GradualDeclineResult, convergence: ConvergenceResult, dimension_divergence: DimensionDivergenceResult }. (5) OUTPUT: include trend_analysis in validation result passed back to main.aisop.json. LoopControl (B3 step1_5) consumes this for exit decisions. trend_health 'critical' → include WARNING in PassRound/FailRound output. (6) LOG: 'TREND ANALYSIS: {trend_health} — decline={decline_detected}, converged={converge_detected}, divergence={diverge_detected}'. SKIP: if completed_rounds.length < 3 or round_state unavailable.
CheckRegression.constraints: STRICT on simulation: any decrease in pass_rate is regression. TOLERANT on sub-dimensions: minor drop (-0.1) acceptable if weighted score maintained or improved. ZERO TOLERANCE on I2 (safety): if I_score drops, always regression. Cross-round trend analysis is advisory — does not override single-round regression verdict but is passed to LoopControl for exit decisions.
CheckRegression.Error: Cannot evaluate -> treat as Regression -> route to AnalyzeRegression. Trend analysis fails -> skip trend, continue with single-round evaluation only.

PassRound.step1: Produce validation_result = { passed: true, quality_delta: weighted_delta, dimension_breakdown: { C: C_delta, I: I_delta, D: D_delta }, regression_detected: false, recommendation: 'Continue to next round.' }.
PassRound.constraints: Include all dimension deltas for caller's FinalReport.
PassRound.Error: Output fails -> return minimal { passed: true }.

AnalyzeRegression.step1: Identify regression root cause: (a) Which dimension(s) regressed? (b) By how much? (c) Was it caused by new nodes (complexity increase)? (d) Was it caused by removed guards (safety decrease)? (e) Was simulation coverage reduced?
AnalyzeRegression.step2: Classify severity: (a) MINOR: single dimension drop < 0.2, weighted maintained. (b) MAJOR: weighted score dropped, or I_score dropped (safety). (c) CRITICAL: simulation pass_rate dropped below 95%, or grade downgraded.
AnalyzeRegression.constraints: Safety (I_score) regression is always MAJOR or CRITICAL.

DecideAction.step1: Based on severity: (a) MINOR -> Recoverable: Creator's ModifyStep could fix this. Route to SuggestFix. (b) MAJOR -> Unrecoverable within this round. Route to FailRound. (c) CRITICAL -> Unrecoverable. Route to FailRound with urgent flag.
DecideAction.constraints: MINOR is the ONLY recoverable severity. MAJOR and CRITICAL always fail the round.

SuggestFix.step1: Produce validation_result = { passed: true, quality_delta: weighted_delta, dimension_breakdown: { C: C_delta, I: I_delta, D: D_delta }, regression_detected: true, regression_severity: 'MINOR', recommendation: 'Round passed with minor regression. Suggest addressing regressed dimension in next round.', fix_suggestions: [string] }.
SuggestFix.step2: Add regressed dimension to planner's priority list for next round.
SuggestFix.constraints: SuggestFix passes the round but flags the regression for future attention. This prevents blocking progress on minor issues.
SuggestFix.Error: Output fails -> return { passed: true, regression_detected: true }.

FailRound.step1: Produce validation_result = { passed: false, quality_delta: weighted_delta, dimension_breakdown: { C: C_delta, I: I_delta, D: D_delta }, regression_detected: true, regression_severity: severity, recommendation: 'Round FAILED. Rollback recommended. Reason: {root_cause}.' }.
FailRound.constraints: Failed round MUST be rolled back by caller. Include enough detail for user to understand why.
FailRound.Error: Output fails -> return minimal { passed: false, recommendation: 'Round failed — rollback.' }.

content.constraints: GLOBAL: (1) Execution failure = automatic round failure. (2) Safety (I_score) regression = always MAJOR+. (3) Simulation pass_rate decrease = always regression. (4) Minor sub-dimension regression is tolerated if weighted score maintained. (5) Conservative defaults: when in doubt, fail the round (safer to rollback than to accumulate quality debt).
content.Error: Unhandled error -> return { passed: false, recommendation: 'Validation error — rollback for safety.' }.
